digest.top_line: AREST: adjuvant RT lifts 3yr LRFS to 89.2% vs 80.9% (HR 0.52) in intermediate-risk resected oral SCC; LC gain only, no OS/DFS benefit at 47mo.
digest.tldr: Head-neck carried the day: AREST, a phase III RCT (N=392), shows adjuvant RT after resection of intermediate-risk pT1-2N0 oral SCC cuts loco-regional recurrence (3yr LRFS 89.2% vs 80.9%, HR 0.52) but doesn't move OS or DFS at 47mo. Reopens the add-vs-omit RT question in early oral cavity; dose/fractionation unreported.

bookmarks.text:
#ASCO26 

🗣️ The AREST trial tackles one of the biggest gray zones in oral cavity cancer.

After adequate surgery in pT1-2N0 OSCC with intermediate-risk features:

✅ Adjuvant RT improved loco-regional control
❌ No OS benefit observed

3-year LRFS:
🔹 89.2% vs 80.9%
🔹 HR 0.52… https://t.co/qsALPFX032 pic.twitter.com/F4XzTMETih

AREST Trial: Adjuvant radiotherapy vs observation
after curative surgery for early-stage (pT1-2NO)
oral squamous cell carcinoma with intermediate-risk features
3-YEAR LOCO-REGIONAL RECURRENCE-FREE SURVIVAL (LRFS)
ADJUVANT RT
89.2%
(95% CI: 84.3% - 93.3%)
HR
0.52
(95% CI: 0.30-0.91)
p = 0.02
OBSERVATION
80.9%
(95% Cl: 74.6% - 86.1%)
Better
loco-regional control
No overall survival
benefit yet
(No significant difference in DFS or OS
at median follow-up of 47.2 months)
* EXPLORATORY FINDING
Signal appears
stronger in
oral tongue tumors
Shared decision-making
remains key.
Multicenter
Phase III RCT
from India
N = 392
(191 RT vs 201
Observation)
Primary endpoint
LRFS
